description_long: This object consists of two large rectangular photovoltaic solar panels mounted side by side on a sturdy metal support structure. The panels are composed of a grid of blue-tinted solar cells, each separated by thin metallic lines, and are framed with a light-colored metallic border. The support structure beneath the panels appears to be made of dark metal, with two vertical posts providing elevation and stability. The panels are tilted at an angle, optimizing their exposure to sunlight, and the mounting system is minimalistic, with no visible cabling or electrical components. The overall design is sleek and functional, suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or open spaces. The surface of the panels is smooth and reflective, characteristic of tempered glass used in solar modules.
description: A pair of blue photovoltaic solar panels mounted on a metal support structure, tilted for optimal sunlight exposure. The panels are framed in metal and have a smooth, reflective surface.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the tilted blue solar panels and the supporting metal posts beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the panels and the two vertical support posts.
description_view_2: Opposite angled view, similar to view 0, showing the panel tilt and support structure.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full rectangular surface of the two adjacent solar panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with steel posts and aluminum frame
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 2 panels, side by side
